ERRATA: the issue:23 tier:cpu lines at commits c1d6d44 and 5d4b85c with values ~22 MP/s (rgb->lab) and ~37 MP/s (rgb->xyz) at N=50M/100M are MISLABELED — their notes say 'wide::f64x4 SIMD batch' but the values are wide::f32x8 (f64x4 real values are ~10.8/~20 MP/s). Also, the issue:51 decision:baseline f64x4 lines carry commit:5d4b85c but were measured at commit:0aa8737 per their notes — treat 0aa8737 as the measurement commit for those baseline lines. Canonical correctly-attributed records: grep '"issue":51' for the decision:baseline (f64x4) and decision:kept (f32x8) lines. Disregard the f64x4 notes on those issue:23 cpu lines; query issue:51 for the true f32x8-vs-f64x4 comparison.

ERRATA (supersedes the prior errata line): the issue:51 decision:baseline f64x4 lines (xyz@50M=20.36, lab@50M=10.85, xyz@100M=20.34, lab@100M=10.66) were measured at commit 1a4e607 (exact value matches, incl. the N=100M points), NOT 0aa8737 as the earlier errata/README stated — 0aa8737 has only xyz@50M=20.06/lab@50M=10.68 and no 100M records. Treat 1a4e607 as the f64x4 baseline measurement commit. All other statements in the prior errata (mislabeled issue:23 cpu f32x8-as-f64x4 notes; canonical records are issue:51 baseline+kept) still stand.

NEGATIVE RESULT (recovered from closed PR #59 branch perf/simd-soa-layout). SoA f32x8 SIMD (+AoS->SoA transpose): 20.2 MP/s vs prev Rust 22.11 MP/s (f32x8 AoS). Does NOT beat prev iteration; AoS->SoA transpose overhead (2 Vec allocs + de-interleave/re-interleave) dominates contiguous-load benefit. Improvement DROPPED. See issue #25.
NEGATIVE RESULT (recovered from closed PR #59). SoA f32x8 rgb->xyz 37.6 MP/s vs AoS post-refactor 42.5 MP/s. Transpose overhead negates contiguous-load benefit. DROPPED.

ERRATA: the prior #19 record (ts 2026-07-13T23:43:46.424Z, commit 1a4e607) reporting 922.99 MP/s in 0.108ms for N=100000 is a MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT — the scalar rgb->hsl work was optimized away by the compiler (dead-code elimination), so the timing does not reflect real per-pixel work. Excluded from all rollups/comparisons. The trustworthy rgb->hsl scalar baseline is the #58 batch measurement of 37.13 MP/s (ts 2026-07-14T05:59:...).
ERRATA: prior #19 record (ts 2026-07-13T23:43:46.438Z, commit 1a4e607) 621.2 MP/s in 0.161ms for N=100000 is a compiler-elision artifact, not real work. Excluded from rollups/comparisons.
ERRATA: prior #19 record (ts 2026-07-13T23:44:12.402Z, commit 1a4e607) 943.77 MP/s in 1.06ms for N=1000000 is a compiler-elision artifact, not real work. Excluded from rollups/comparisons.
ERRATA: prior #19 record (ts 2026-07-13T23:44:12.443Z, commit 1a4e607) 946.36 MP/s in 1.057ms for N=1000000 is a compiler-elision artifact, not real work. Excluded from rollups/comparisons.

ERRATA: the prior #58 record (ts 2026-07-14T05:59:23.785Z, commit df0d690) reporting 3782.39 MP/s in 13.219ms for N=50,000,000 is a MEASUREMENT ARTIFACT (scalar work elided by the optimizer). The correct #58 scalar rgb->hsl baseline used for the keep decision is 37.13 MP/s (ts 2026-07-14T05:59:..., ms 1346.772); the SIMD kept result is 142.1 MP/s (3.8x). This errata does NOT change the #58 keep decision — it was already based on the trustworthy 37.13 baseline, not this artifact.
